problem: On each of the first three days of January, there is a $\frac{1}{3}$ chance that it will snow where Bob lives. On each of the next four days, there is a $\frac{1}{4}$ chance that it will snow. What is the probability that it snows at least once during the first week of January?

classified_questions.question: What is the event that we are interested in calculating the probability of?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many days are there in the first week of January that are being considered in this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What are the probabilities of it snowing on the first three days and on the next four days?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the probability of it snowing at least once during the first week of January?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why do we subtract the probability of no snow from 1 to find the probability of at least one snow day?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we use a different method to calculate the probability of it snowing at least once during the first week of January? If so, what would that method be?
classified_questions.label: b
